report_name: Vishing Campaign Tricks Users into Enrolling Attacker Passkeys to Hack Microsoft 365 Accounts

threat_actors.name: O-UNC-066
threat_actors.description: A sophisticated threat actor targeting Microsoft 365 users with a voice phishing (vishing) campaign. The actor, tracked by Okta, impersonates IT staff to trick users into enrolling an attacker-controlled passkey, enabling persistent, passwordless account access for data extortion against technology, healthcare, automotive, and aviation sectors.
threat_actors.goals: Gain persistent, passwordless access to victim Microsoft 365 accounts for data extortion.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Voice
attack_patterns.description: The attacker calls an employee, posing as a member of the internal IT helpdesk, to socially engineer them into visiting a phishing site under a plausible pretext.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Link
attack_patterns.description: The attacker directs the victim to a high-quality phishing website that replicates the legitimate Microsoft Entra ID login portal to facilitate the fraudulent passkey enrollment.

attack_patterns.name: Device Registration
attack_patterns.description: The core of the attack involves deceiving the user into going through the FIDO2/WebAuthn enrollment process on a phishing site, which registers the attacker's device or passkey to the victim's account.

attack_patterns.name: Use Alternate Authentication Material
attack_patterns.description: The attacker gains persistent, passwordless access by tricking the user into enrolling an attacker-controlled FIDO2 passkey, which serves as a form of alternate authentication material.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: After gaining access, the attackers use their persistent access to exfiltrate sensitive data from the user's email, SharePoint, and OneDrive for data extortion schemes.

tools.name: Phishing Kit
tools.description: A high-quality phishing kit was used to create a pixel-perfect replica of the Microsoft Entra ID login portal to deceive users during the passkey enrollment process.

infrastructure.name: Entra ID Phishing Portal
infrastructure.description: A phishing website, often using a typosquatted domain, that is a pixel-perfect replica of an organization's Microsoft Entra ID login portal, used to trick users into enrolling an attacker-controlled passkey.

indicators.name: Suspicious Microsoft Entra ID Passkey Enrollment
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Microsoft Entra ID Passkey Enrollment
id: 2b8a1c9d-4f5e-4b1a-8c7d-9e0f1a2b3c4d
status: experimental
description: Detects the enrollment of a new FIDO2 security key or passkey in Microsoft Entra ID. This activity is a key step in a vishing campaign where attackers trick users into registering an attacker-controlled device.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/10/vishing-campaign-fake-entra-passkey-enrollment-hacks-microsoft-365
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-10
tags:
  - attack.t1098.005
level: high
logsource:
  product: azure
  service: auditlogs
detection:
  selection:
    OperationName:
      - 'Register security key'
      - 'User registered security info'
      - 'Register FIDO2 security key'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the enrollment of a new FIDO2 security key or passkey in Microsoft Entra ID, which is a key indicator of the O-UNC-066 vishing campaign.

relationships.source_name: Suspicious Microsoft Entra ID Passkey Enrollment
Suspicious Microsoft Entra ID Passkey Enrollment
